mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which directly improves near-term corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during monetary tightening periods, or signal direction reverses when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction across multiple market cycles (Rank IC consistently near zero).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening and erodes real corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand strength, which elevates equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annually, or interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening cycles, or signal accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample validation.
